methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $60M - $500M.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

private.name: Tenna Systems
private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (Seed), post-money $60M

data.narrative:
Rivet Industries is a seed-stage defense and security company founded by Dave Marra. The comparable universe comprises five private peers operating in the same sector and stage, with a broader comp set of ten private companies and two public proxies (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) used for directional context.

The peer-median method serves as the primary valuation approach given data availability. The analysis examined five same-sector, same/adjacent-stage peers: Stark Defence ($500M Series A), Vatn Systems ($300M Series A), Ares Industries ($150M Series A), NODA AI ($125M Series A), and Tenna Systems ($60M Seed). The median valuation across this set is $150M, with a range spanning $60M to $500M. This peer-median directly implies a $150M valuation for Rivet.

The triangulated analysis, constrained by a single applicable methodology, yields a valuation range of $128M to $172M, centered on $150M. The dispersion reflects inherent variation in the peer set: the presence of later-stage Series A companies (Stark Defence, Vatn Systems, Ares) at the higher end creates upside anchoring, while Tenna Systems—the only true seed-stage comparable—grounds the lower bound at $60M. Key uncertainties include Rivet's relative progress versus peers, product-market fit validation, and customer traction, none of which are quantified in available data. A more granular valuation would require revenue multiples, growth rates, or customer acquisition metrics.
